records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

records.text: 00_session_startup.md: New project: ask what building, propose name, create dirs, copy templates, detect Rhino level, collect refs, offer interview. Resume: read project_prompt+log, identify last phase, confirm next. Phase exec: read INDEX+phase prompt+project_prompt, project overrides system defaults. OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

records.text: 00_session_startup.md: New project: ask what building, propose name, create dirs, copy templates, detect Rhino level, collect refs, offer interview. Resume: read project_prompt+log, identify last phase, confirm next. Phase exec: read INDEX+phase prompt+project_prompt, project overrides system defaults. OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

Resume: read project_prompt+log, identify last phase, confirm next. Phase exec: read INDEX+phase prompt+project_prompt, project overrides system defaults. OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

records.text: 00_session_startup.md: New project: ask what building, propose name, create dirs, copy templates, detect Rhino level, collect refs, offer interview. Resume: read project_prompt+log, identify last phase, confirm next. Phase exec: read INDEX+phase prompt+project_prompt, project overrides system defaults. OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

records.text: 00_session_startup.md: New project: ask what building, propose name, create dirs, copy templates, detect Rhino level, collect refs, offer interview. Resume: read project_prompt+log, identify last phase, confirm next. Phase exec: read INDEX+phase prompt+project_prompt, project overrides system defaults. OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. records.text: 00_session_startup.md: New project: ask what building, propose name, create dirs, copy templates, detect Rhino level, collect refs, offer interview. Resume: read project_prompt+log, identify last phase, confirm next. Phase exec: read INDEX+phase prompt+project_prompt, project overrides system defaults. OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

records.text: README.md summary: AEC CPTX Demo is an AI arch-viz pipeline. Agent reads design brief, builds Rhino 3D model via MCP, exports to Blender for rendering, optional ComfyUI post. 13 phases: 00 startup, 01 config, 02 site prep (Rhino), 03 massing (Rhino), 04-05 floorplans (Rhino), 06 detailing (Rhino), 07 export/validate, 08 lighting+camera (Blender), 09 materials (Blender), 10 test render, 11 final render, 12 layer reveal, 13 sun study. Key principles: derive don't redraw, one object per MCP call, metadata propagates via User Text, backup before every change. Active project: cliff_house_02. Prereqs: Hermes Agent,

records.text: 00_session_startup.md: New project: ask what building, propose name, create dirs, copy templates, detect Rhino level, collect refs, offer interview. Resume: read project_prompt+log, identify last phase, confirm next. Phase exec: read INDEX+phase prompt+project_prompt, project overrides system defaults. OBS: Hermes ONLY writes current_stage.json never controls recording. Phase names: site_prep massing detailing export materials lighting camera render session. Screenshot: capture as-is never manipulate viewport. Compass: N/E/S/W then perspective leave in perspective. Facade: named side=ALL elements ALL floors.

OBJ export from Rhino to Blender workflow (proven June 2026): Never use _-Export command via MCP (blocks slot). Instead write OBJ manually from Rhino Python: mesh all Breps via rg.Mesh.CreateFromBrep(), write v/f lines with mm-to-meter division (coord/1000). Group objects by material tag using usemtl directives. Write companion MTL file. Import in Blender with bpy.ops.wm.obj_import(forward_axis=NEGATIVE_Z, up_axis=Y). OBJ imports as single mesh - use bpy.ops.mesh.separate(type=MATERIAL) to split by material slot. Replace .001 MTL materials with pre-created PBR materials by matching base name. Set rotation_mode=XYZ on all mesh objects immediately after import. Organize into collections by material-to-collection map.

OBJ export from Rhino to Blender workflow (proven June 2026): Never use _-Export command via MCP (blocks slot). Instead write OBJ manually from Rhino Python: mesh all Breps via rg.Mesh.CreateFromBrep(), write v/f lines with mm-to-meter division (coord/1000). Group objects by material tag using usemtl directives. Write companion MTL file. Import in Blender with bpy.ops.wm.obj_import(forward_axis=NEGATIVE_Z, up_axis=Y). OBJ imports as single mesh - use bpy.ops.mesh.separate(type=MATERIAL) to split by material slot. Replace .001 MTL materials with pre-created PBR materials by matching base name. Set rotation_mode=XYZ on all mesh objects immediately after import. Organize into collections by material-to-collection map.

Blender OBJ import creates duplicate materials with .001 suffix when materials of the same name already exist. Fix: iterate material_slots on imported object, replace each .001 material with the original PBR version by name matching (strip .001 suffix, look up base name in bpy.data.materials). Then remove orphan .001 materials. The separate-by-material creates one object per material slot. Rename objects using mat_to_name map for clarity. All objects start at origin (0,0,0) which is correct since OBJ vertices already contain absolute positions.

CRITICAL: OBJ import axis mapping for Rhino-to-Blender. When exporting OBJ from Rhino Python with raw coordinates (v x y z), Rhino uses X=right Y=forward Z=up which is the SAME as Blender's convention. Use bpy.ops.wm.obj_import(forward_axis='Y', up_axis='Z') for identity mapping. Using forward_axis='NEGATIVE_Z' up_axis='Y' (Blender default for 3ds Max convention) swaps Y and Z axes, making the building lie on its side. Symptom: scene Z range equals the Rhino Y range (north-south span) and scene Y range equals Rhino Z range (vertical). Always verify scene bounding box immediately after OBJ import before proceeding with camera/lighting.

records.text: Phase 0 (Project Config) complete for vp_studio_01. Created 17 project subdirectories, 62 Rhino layers, set units to meters with 0.001 tolerance. Built initial site geometry: 120m asphalt lot, 75x87m concrete apron, stage house envelope (37x52m, 18m tall with parapets to 19m), loading dock with roll-up doors and drive-on ramp, 270-degree LED volume (20m wide, 12m deep, 7m tall elliptical arc centered at 0,-8), rigging grid (13 trusses at 15m), 2-story support wing (14x52m, 8.5m tall), glazing (south entrance, brain bar window, gallery glass, support wing strips), entry canopy and signage. 45 total objects. Coordinate system: origin at stage center, +Y=North (dock), -Y=South (entry), +X=East (support wing). Checkpoint saved as base_model_checkpoint_20260629_1143.3dm. Phase 1 (Site Prep) is N/A for this flat-lot project.

VP Studio 01 demo contract: virtual production stage facility for entertainment industry audience. Building is a dark charcoal industrial stage house (37x52m, 18m tall) with 270-degree LED volume (20m x 12m x 7m), rigging grid at 15m, 2-story support wing (14x52m) with brain bar control room, client gallery, scenic workshop, and technical infrastructure. Hero shot from SW looking NE with LED wall blue glow visible through open loading dock. Late afternoon California light. Materials: standing-seam metal panels (dark charcoal), cement board, glass with dark bronze frames, matte black for LED wall and rigging. Demo callouts: LED wall layout, brain bar sightlines, acoustic isolation, 2MW electrical, rigging grid, rear-serviceable panels, dedicated processor and render rooms.

Hero shot from SW looking NE with LED wall blue glow visible through open loading dock. Late afternoon California light. records.text: Phase 3-5 (Detailing/Floorplan) complete for vp_studio_01. Added 72 objects (202->274 total). Checkpoint: base_model_checkpoint_20260629_1200.3dm.

Full Furnishing phase complete for vp_studio_01. Added 177 objects (274->451 total). New geometry organized by category: STAGE PRODUCTION (41 objects) — 3 camera pedestals (base+column+head each) on LED platform at positions (0,-4), (-5,-2), (5,-2); jib/crane arm at (0,9); video village with table, 4 monitors, 3 director chairs; 8 C-stands around stage; 4 apple box stacks; hero vehicle set piece (car body + roof). SUPPORT WING GF FURNITURE (32 objects) — scenic workshop: 3 work tables + wall shelving; grip storage: 2 rolling carts + shelving; camera prep: workbench + 3 Pelican cases; LED processor room: 4 equipment racks + workstation desk; server room: 6 server racks (2 rows of 3); hair/makeup: 3 vanity stations with mirrors + styling chair; 2 green room couches; kitchenette counter. UPPER FLOOR FURNITURE (44 objects) — brain bar: 8-position console desk with 8 monitors + large reference monitor + 4 operator chairs; client gallery: 4 seats + coffee table; 3 offices each with desk, monitor, chair; conference room: large table, 8 chairs, wall display; bullpen: 6 workstation desks. ELECTRICAL/MEP (12 objects) — main switchgear (2MW), 4 PDU panels, transformer pad (exterior), generator hookup pad, cam-lok panel, 4 stage power distro boxes. PRODUCTION LIGHTING (20 objects) — 4 key/fill LED panels on yokes from rigging grid, 4 backlight fixtures on yokes, 4 practical accent lights on service corridor wall. LANDSCAPING (28 objects) — 12 trees (trunk + canopy each), 2 entry planters, monument sign (base + face). Checkpoint: base_model_checkpoint_20260629_1210.3dm.

records.text: Phase 6 (Blender Export + Render) complete for vp_studio_01. Pipeline: (1) Tagged 451 Rhino objects with 18 VP-specific PBR material tags, (2) Manual OBJ writer from Rhino Python — 1.0MB, 21K verts, 15K faces, 451 groups in meters, (3) Blender 5.1 import via BlenderMCP TCP, separated by material into 18 objects in 7 collections (Site, Structure, Glazing, Production, Furnishing, Vehicles, Emissive), (4) Full Cycles PBR material setup: charcoal standing-seam metal (metallic 1.0, rough 0.18), dark anodized aluminum mullions, glass BSDF, LED panel emissive (strength 25, purple/blue), screen emissive (strength 15), warm oak wood, dark fabric, sealed concrete, dark asphalt, foliage green, (5) Golden hour lighting: sun at 12.0 energy low SW angle, 500W fill sky bounce, 5000W LED dock spill (purple), 4000W south entry LED spill, 1200W brain bar glow, 2x entrance accent lights, gradient sky world (golden horizon to deep blue zenith), (6) Hero camera at (-45,-55,30) looking at (5,0,8), 35mm lens, (7) Test render v1 too dark — boosted all lights, moved LED glow outside walls, (8) Test render v2 improved — golden hour reads on metal panels, LED glow visible, (9) Final render 1920x1080 256 samples GPU. Artifacts: renders/vp_studio_01_final_render.png, blender_assets/vp_studio_01_scene.blend, blender_assets/vp_studio_01_export.obj.

Blender Export and Render (vp_studio_01): Exported 342 Rhino objects to OBJ (1.9MB), imported to Blender 5.1. Fixed axis rotation (Rhino Z-up to Blender). 6 camera angles rendered at 1920x1080 128 samples Cycles GPU: exterior SW hero, exterior NW loading dock, exterior S entrance, interior LED volume, interior brain bar, interior loading dock. Materials assigned by position heuristic: cladding, concrete, glass, LED wall (emission), steel, roof. Sun light from southwest, warm blue sky background. OBJ axis swap required 180-degree X rotation after import.

Blender Export and Render (vp_studio_01): Exported 342 Rhino objects to OBJ (1.9MB), imported to Blender 5.1. Fixed axis rotation (Rhino Z-up to Blender). 6 camera angles rendered at 1920x1080 128 samples Cycles GPU: exterior SW hero, exterior NW loading dock, exterior S entrance, interior LED volume, interior brain bar, interior loading dock. Materials assigned by position heuristic: cladding, concrete, glass, LED wall (emission), steel, roof. Sun light from southwest, warm blue sky background. OBJ axis swap required 180-degree X rotation after import.

ComfyUI Enhancement (vp_studio_01): Rendered 6 depth maps from Blender using ShaderNodeCameraData View Distance with Map Range normalization (5m near white, 150m far black). Zero bounces, 1 sample, transparent film, world strength 0. 16-bit PNG. ComfyUI SDXL img2img with ControlNet depth conditioning (controlnet-depth-sdxl-1.0). Key finding: V2 plain img2img at low denoise 0.28-0.35 produced visually identical images across seeds — too flat. V3 with ControlNet depth at denoise 0.42-0.50 still too similar. V4 at denoise 0.65-0.70 with CN strength 0.72-0.78 and per-seed prompt variation (golden hour/blue hour/overcast for exteriors, sci-fi/desert/storm for LED volume) produced dramatically different results. Higher denoise is essential when using ControlNet — the depth maps hold geometry but diffusion needs room to actually paint. Local ComfyUI required PyTorch nightly cu128 for Blackwell GPU compatibility (RTX PRO 6000 sm_120 not supported by torch 2.6 cu124).

ComfyUI Enhancement (vp_studio_01): Rendered 6 depth maps from Blender using ShaderNodeCameraData View Distance with Map Range normalization (5m near white, 150m far black). Zero bounces, 1 sample, transparent film, world strength 0. 16-bit PNG. ComfyUI SDXL img2img with ControlNet depth conditioning (controlnet-depth-sdxl-1.0). Key finding: V2 plain img2img at low denoise 0.28-0.35 produced visually identical images across seeds — too flat. V3 with ControlNet depth at denoise 0.42-0.50 still too similar. V4 at denoise 0.65-0.70 with CN strength 0.72-0.78 and per-seed prompt variation (golden hour/blue hour/overcast for exteriors, sci-fi/desert/storm for LED volume) produced dramatically different results. Higher denoise is essential when using ControlNet — the depth maps hold geometry but diffusion needs room to actually paint. Local ComfyUI required PyTorch nightly cu128 for Blackwell GPU compatibility (RTX PRO 6000 sm_120 not supported by torch 2.6 cu124).

ComfyUI Setup on this machine: ComfyUI at C:/Users/test/ComfyUI uses Hermes venv Python 3.11. Required installing: sqlalchemy, filelock, aiosqlite, alembic, comfy-aimdo, safetensors, transformers, tokenizers, sentencepiece, einops, scipy, psutil, torchsde, av, comfyui-workflow-templates. PyTorch 2.6 cu124 does NOT support Blackwell sm_120 — must use nightly cu128: pip install --pre torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/nightly/cu128. numpy compiled for 3.12 broke 3.11 venv — required force reinstall of numpy. SDXL checkpoint at sd_xl_base_1.0.safetensors, ControlNet at controlnet-depth-sdxl-1.0. No depth preprocessor node available in this ComfyUI install — must use Blender-rendered depth maps directly as ControlNet input.
